title: plectranthus monostachyus subsp. marrubiifolius
intro: Plectranthus monostachyus subsp. marrubiifolius, also known as Vervain of the Cape or Cape Vervain, is a perennial plant species native to South Africa. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can be attributed to the presence of ursolic acid and oleanolic acid. The health benefits of Plectranthus monostachyus subsp. marrubiifolius include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and provide relief from respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, the leaves of this plant are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be used to treat various ailments, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
